proof_sketch: batch_size=128, stride=255. For any thread tid (0..255), loop condition i < batch_size is true only when i = tid (since tid < 128). Then i += 255 >= 128, so no further iterations. Original stride=256 also gives one iteration per thread. Both access the same set of elements in the same order, leading to identical shared_sum entries and final result.

  "reason_category": "value_insensitive",

Original stride=256 also gives one iteration per thread. Both access the same set of elements in the same order, leading to identical shared_sum entries and final result.",
  "survival_reason": "The mutation changes the stride in the final reduction kernel from 256 to 255. However, because the fixed batch size (128) is less than both strides, each thread executes exactly one loop iteration in both versions. The elements loaded and accumulated are identical per thread (batch_means[tid]), and the subsequent shared-memory reduction proceeds identically. No floating-point order difference arises because each thread's local sum is unchanged. Therefore, under the fixed-shape contract (batch_size=128), the mutant produces bitwise-identical results to the original for all possible input values.",
